title: Left Rotate Array by K Places

content.problemStatement: Given an array of integers, rotating array of elements by k elements either left or right.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain an array of integers and an integer k. The first line will contain the number of elements in the array (n), the second line will contain the array elements, and the third line will contain the value of k.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the array after rotating it to the left or right by k positions.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a temporary array to store the elements that need to be rotated. Then, we can shift the remaining elements and copy the stored elements to their new positions.

code.Python3:
class Solution:
    # Rotate the array to the right by k positions
    def rotateRight(self, arr, k):
        n = len(arr)
        if n == 0:
            return

        k %= n

        # Store last k elements
        temp = arr[-k:]

        # Shift the remaining elements
        for i in range(n - k - 1, -1, -1):
            arr[i + k] = arr[i]

        # Copy stored elements to the front
        for i in range(k):
            arr[i] = temp[i]

    # Rotate the array to the left by k positions
    def rotateLeft(self, arr, k):
        n = len(arr)
        if n == 0:
            return

        k %= n

        # Store first k elements
        temp = arr[:k]

        # Shift remaining elements
        for i in range(k, n):
            arr[i - k] = arr[i]

        # Copy stored elements to the end
        for i in range(k):
            arr[n - k + i] = temp[i]


# Driver code
sol = Solution()

arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
k = 2
sol.rotateRight(arr, k)
print("Array after right rotation:", arr)

arr2 = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
sol.rotateLeft(arr2, k)
print("Array after left rotation:", arr2)
